Instructions: Read 5–10 words, wait 1 minute, then try to recall them.
Write numbers out of order and rearrange them into ascending order.

Complete a crossword puzzle using given clues.
Fill a 9x9 grid so each number appears once per row, column, and box.
Match names with faces or objects and recall later.
List words starting with each letter of the alphabet.
Solve simple math problems in your head.
Name as many items as possible in a category (e.g., fruits) in 1 minute.
Look at items on a tray, cover them, then recall as many as possible.
Spell common or challenging words aloud or on paper.
Assemble a puzzle to improve spatial thinking.
Read a paragraph aloud to improve focus and comprehension.
Identify patterns in numbers or shapes.
Study a simple map, then redraw it from memory.
Solve simple riddles or brain teasers.
Listen to a song and recall lyrics or melody.
Write a short summary of your day.
Describe an object in detail from memory.
Repeat a sequence of numbers or words backward.
Explain the meaning of common sayings.
Match colors with objects or images.
Count backward from 100 by 3s or 7s.
Look at an image and create a short story.
Unscramble mixed-up words.
Complete analogies (e.g., Cat is to Kitten as Dog is to ?).
Plan events on a calendar for the week.
Recall historical facts or personal memories.
Explain how to get from one place to another.
Draw shapes from memory.
List synonyms for common words.
List antonyms for given words.
Recall steps of a favorite recipe.
Group items into categories.
Estimate how long a task takes.
Listen to a short passage and summarize it.
Create a chain where each word starts with the last letter of the previous word.
Compare two images and find differences.
Solve simple real-life math problems.
Identify emotions from facial expressions.
Create lists (e.g., groceries) from memory.
Memorize and recite short poems.
Answer general knowledge questions.
Learn and recall new words from another language.
Look at a picture, then recall details.
Sort cards or objects by rules.
Solve logic puzzles.
Alternate between two tasks (e.g., counting and naming).
Trace patterns or shapes carefully.
